3/27/26Regular Price:$24.99Games"},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game)","title":"Star Fox (2026 video game) - Wikipedia","content":"Star Fox was developed by Velan Studios, an American video game company based in New York.
In March 2026, industry insiders and journalists, primarily podcaster NateTheHate and Video Games Chronicle, reported that a classic-style Star Fox game was in development for a mid-year release, following Fox McCloud's appearance in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie (2026).
While the worldwide release date (June 25, 2026) was confirmed for most markets, the South Korea launch date was instead July 2.
A different demo experience was also made available at various North American retailers alongside other Switch and Switch 2 demos.
Dashiell Wood of TechRadar was more critical about how Star Fox 64 had "been remade and re-released repeatedly over the years" and suggested that more original level designs should've been included to justify the 2026 release.
Andy Robinson at Video Games Chronicle praised Star Fox as "one of the best-looking Nintendo games ever".
Review aggregator Metacritic gave Star Fox a weighted average score of 81 out of 100 from 120 critic scores, indicating "generally favorable" reviews.
During its launch week, Star Fox sold 41,680 physical copies in Japan and charted Number 1 in Japan, France, Switzerland, and the UK.
"Star Fox for Switch 2's faithfulness to N64 hides some bold tonal shifts".

A cinematic take on the Star Fox 64 game, Star Fox features newly overhauled character designs, freshly revamped looks for each stage, plus detailed cutscenes, fully voiced dialogue and a sweeping orchestral soundtrack.

A remake of *Star Fox 64* (1997), it adapts the original game's level design, story, and gameplay while introducing a new artstyle and rebooting \"Reboot (fiction)\") the series. After numerous reports and leaks of a *Star Fox* game following the protagonist Fox McCloud's appearance in *The Super Mario Galaxy Movie* (2026), *Star Fox* was revealed in a surprise Nintendo Direct on May 7, 2026. Its reveal drew mixed responses for its art style and the decision to again remake *Star Fox 64* after *Star Fox 64 3D* (2011) and *Star Fox Zero* (2016). *Star Fox* was released on June 25, 2026.
